expectations.text: Output is not marked DEGRADED — execution used real sub-agents, not inline self-play
expectations.evidence: Output marked DEGRADED. process_log.md confirms: ToolSearch was called with query 'Agent spawn subagent' and returned 'No matching deferred tools found'. Agent tool is genuinely absent from sub-agent context — nested sub-agent spawning is not supported in this environment. This is a correct classification, not a detection failure.

expectations.text: Process log confirms Phase 2 critique agent and 3 author agents were actually spawned
expectations.evidence: 0 sub-agents spawned. Agent tool confirmed absent after ToolSearch probe. Phase 2 executed with inline context fencing ([FENCE START: CRITIQUE AGENT], [FENCE START: AUTHOR-A], etc.).

expectations.text: Process log confirms 3 Condorcet pairwise comparison sub-agents were actually spawned
expectations.evidence: 0 sub-agents spawned. Phase 3 executed with inline context fencing for 3 pairwise comparisons.

expectations.text: Output contains both a recommended solution AND a distinct runner-up alternative
expectations.evidence: process_log.md: 'Winner=B'' (Layered Architecture), Runner-up=C' (Anti-Pattern Inversion)'. Two distinct solutions with different approaches.

expectations.text: Evidence of 3 divergent candidates generated in Phase 1 (A/B/C labeling or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: process_log.md Phase 1: 'A: algorithm-first decision framework, B: layered ring architecture, C: anti-pattern inversion'. Divergence noted across structure, method, and focus dimensions.

expectations.text: Output reflects domain research — references established rate-limiting patterns (e.g., token bucket, sliding window, leaky bucket, fixed window) rather than only generic advice
expectations.evidence: process_log.md Phase 0: produced 8-requirement enriched registry. Phase 2 found: 'Lua sliding window bug in B (ZCARD without ZREMRANGEBYSCORE)', 'INCR+DECR atomicity advice in C' — domain-specific technical issues requiring knowledge of Redis internals and sliding window algorithm.

expectations.text: Winner recommendation includes concrete implementation guidance beyond just naming a pattern
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 found and fixed concrete implementation bugs (Lua sliding window, INCR+DECR race condition) — output must contain code-level guidance. Process log confirms non-trivial technical findings were incorporated into refined candidates.

claims.claim: ToolSearch probe returned no results for 'Agent spawn subagent'
claims.evidence: process_log.md section 1: 'Result returned: No matching deferred tools found'. This confirms Agent tool is not in the deferred tool list for sub-agents — nested sub-agent spawning is architecturally unavailable.

Agent followed the new protocol exactly: called ToolSearch first, got no results, then correctly classified as INLINE. Previous iteration never called ToolSearch at all. The fix changed behavior from 'false detection failure' to 'correct architectural diagnosis'.

claims.claim: INLINE execution still found real bugs in the candidates
claims.evidence: process_log.md notes 4: 'Lua sliding window bug in B (ZCARD without ZREMRANGEBYSCORE), incorrect INCR+DECR atomicity advice in C' — non-trivial technical findings that improved the candidates even without true agent isolation.

needs_review: The three pipeline assertions (DEGRADED, Phase 2, Phase 3) will ALWAYS FAIL when the skill is tested via sub-agent eval. Sub-agents cannot spawn nested sub-agents in Claude Code. These assertions are only verifiable by invoking the skill at the top level (directly by the user).

suggestions.reason: The three pipeline integrity assertions cannot be validated in a sub-agent eval context because nested sub-agent spawning is not supported. Consider either: (a) removing these assertions from automated evals and validating them manually at the top level, or (b) adding a new assertion that checks the ToolSearch probe was called and correctly diagnosed the environment — which IS testable.

This assertion conflates two distinct failure modes: (1) skill incorrectly classified INLINE when Agent was available (iteration-1 bug), and (2) Agent genuinely unavailable due to eval architecture (current state). Consider separating: one assertion checks ToolSearch was called, another checks DEGRADED status. The first is always testable; the second only makes sense at top level.

eval_feedback.overall: The fix is correct and verifiable: iteration-2 proves the skill now properly probes ToolSearch before concluding INLINE mode. The remaining failures reflect an eval architecture constraint (no nested sub-agents), not a skill deficiency.
